metadata.title: Incident Triage Surface
metadata.subtitle: Packet fixture for browser/runtime validation
metadata.description: A packet focused on runtime hardening: shader startup, browser validation, host bridge churn, and packet diagnostics share one surface.
watchFor: The claim should remain central while operational evidence and warnings orbit without collapsing into a rigid stack.
Lower-confidence risk nodes should stay warmer and softer than the cold validation path.
Packet-driven figures and citations should attach to evidence instead of becoming sidebars.

guideSteps.label: Main hypothesis
guideSteps.description: This is the operational claim the surface is trying to stabilize.

guideSteps.label: Validation chain
guideSteps.description: The browser validation path should stay adjacent to the claim and its proof nodes.

guideSteps.label: Host churn risk
guideSteps.description: This soft risk node should remain legible without overpowering the claim.

guideSteps.label: Telemetry figure
guideSteps.description: This figure is first-class evidence, not a detached screenshot.

tasks.title: Trace the validation path
tasks.prompt: Follow the claim through browser validation, packet diagnostics, and a supporting citation.

tasks.title: Find the unstable operational region
tasks.prompt: Inspect the host churn warning path without losing the main claim.

tasks.title: Explain why the packet warning sits there
tasks.prompt: Select the packet warning node and confirm the solver narrative is still legible.

claim.title: Runtime Hardening
claim.statement: A solver-backed interface only becomes infrastructure when browser validation, packet ingestion, and explainability survive the same runtime surface.

answerBlocks.text: The browser loop now proves WebGPU startup, shader compilation, and runtime readiness instead of relying on screenshots alone.
Packet warnings should stay visible but bounded. Diagnostics belong to the data plane, not to ad-hoc console archaeology.

evidence.text: Headless browser validation now waits for explicit runtime readiness and rejects shader startup regressions.
Knowledge packets are normalized before scene ingest, so malformed targets become warnings instead of silent layout corruption.
Scene normalization rejects duplicate nodes and drops dangling relations before the solver runs.
DOM overlays now update on a throttled cadence, reducing overlay churn without severing explainability.
A fixture catalog makes packet ingest testable across multiple knowledge surfaces instead of one hand-authored sample.

contradictions.text: The host bridge can still dominate cost if overlays rebuild too eagerly around focus and selection changes.
External packet URLs remain risky if teams skip offline validation and ship malformed semantic payloads.
If diagnostics stay buried in tooling, operators will still read the surface as a magical black box.
